mission.title: stacker: Copy imports into layer's rootfs
mission.description:
Copy them into the specified destination
otherwise copy them into layer's rootfs "/"
Preserves links, executability, times.
Don't preserve user/grop ownership and perms.
closes #83

resolution.summary:
A couple of thoughts here:

1. it would be nice if we still bind mounted things in the default configuration (i.e. just `import: foo`); this patch as-is doubles the i/o for an import, copying it once to .stacker and again into the rootfs. Perhaps that means this should be a separate key, like overlay_dirs: in the original issue, so the UX isn't too confusing?

2. It would be really nice if these would just get generated as an OCI layer straight into the output, so that if they didn't change but the contents of the run: section did, you wouldn't get this additional delta.

Let me know if those don't make sense.
steps: it would be nice if we still bind mounted things in the default configuration (i.e. just `import: foo`); this patch as-is doubles the i/o for an import, copying it once to .stacker and again into the rootfs. Perhaps that means this should be a separate key, like overlay_dirs: in the original issue, so the UX isn't too confusing?

It would be really nice if these would just get generated as an OCI layer straight into the output, so that if they didn't change but the contents of the run: section did, you wouldn't get this additional delta.
